rulings.Case Title: CONS. OF ROBERTO T. REYNA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/15/26. (No earlier date available.) (1) A second amended first and final account must be filed that includes the additional accounting for the period subsequent to the date of death. Otherwise, this pleading caption is erroneous, as it is not the final accounting. (The period subsequent to the date of death does not have to be until the date assets are transferred to the personal representative.) (2) The "supplemental declaration" filed on 10/1/25 also refers to a supplemental accounting following the date of death to be filed as a separately noticed petition, while referring to "this final account." (A separately noticed petition should not be required and would be an unnecessary cost to the estate.) (3) The "supplemental declaration" filed on 7/11/25 did not have a copy of the Letters appointing the personal representative attached, as asserted in the "supplemental declaration" filed on 10/1/25. (4) The "supplemental declaration" filed on 10/1/25 now includes a prayer that appears to amend the prayer in the amended petition. Attorney fees and the period services were rendered differ from the fees requested and the period of legal services in the amended petition. (5) Additional anticipated fees or costs will not be ordered if this is not a final account. (6) Who are the beneficiaries of the deceased conservatee's estate? They must be clearly identified and must receive notice. The ex parte petition to increase bond was just assigned to the examiner on 1/21/26. An accounting cannot be approved if bond is insufficient. Deadline is 4/1/26 for providing courtesy copies to the assigned examiner of the second amended first and final account, proof of service of the second amended first and final account, and copies of bank statements verifying the assets on hand. Any difference between the assets shown in the I&A or accouting and the bank statements must be reconciled. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF YUK SHO WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/14/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of the Notice of Hearing has been filed. (3) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) Proof of payment of the assessment fee must be filed before an accounting can be approved. (5) The Partial No. 1 I&A filed on 8/29/25 showed assets totaling $473,337.85, including $27,760.85 in cash, but the accounting begins with $27,692.39 in cash without any explanation. (6) Even if $27,692.39 is the correct amount of cash at the beginning of the accounting period, then the Summary of Account does not balance. (7) No Final I&A has been filed. (8) There is no schedule showing the assets on hand at the beginning of the accounting. [Prob. C. Section 1061(a)(1).] (9) Detailed schedules should have at least four columns, showing the date, the source or payee, the description of the receipt or disbursement, and the amount. (10) Was there an order authorizing the payment of "living expenses" (to whom?)? (11) Assets on hand at the end of the accounting must show the value of assets at their carry value, and an additional schedule must show the estimated market value of the assets. [Prob. C. Sections 1061(1)(10) and 1063(a).] (12) A change in the market value should be reported on an informational schedule, not on a schedule of "market gains." Probate accountings are cash, not accrual, accountings. (13) The bank statement for the period encompassing the beginning date of the accounting period begins with a balance of $26,908.16 as of 3/28/24. The I&A reported cash to be $27,760.85 as of 4/5/24. No reconciliation is shown. (14) The bank statement for the month ending 4/28/25 shows a balance of $34,837.02. The accounting shows $33,860.71 as of 4/24/25. No reconciliation is shown. (15) The I&A appraisals were as of the date of appointment, 4/5/24, but the accounting begins on 8/22/24. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/30/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RUBLY FAMILY TRUST DATED JULY 20, 1988, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee To Fill Vacancy
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/04/2026. Issues necessitating a continuance: 1) A petition involving a trust must state the names and addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries (CRC 7.902). The instant petition does not provide petitioner's address or list the Christian Scientist Church. 2) Paragraph 25 of the instant petition suggests that the ongoing administration of the trust is in San Francisco. Please clarify if this statement is based on anything other than the location of an office for Raymond James Financial. 3) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court. Any party lodging a proposed order in a contested matter must file proof of service of the proposed order on all parties entitled to notice of the petition. (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A.4). Please address the above issues and provide courtesy copies of all filings to this examiner no later than 02/18/2026 to avoid additional continuance.
